stories.title: Knicks 121-108 Cavaliers — ECF Game 3, Knicks Lead 3-0
stories.angle: No team in NBA history has ever come back from a 3-0 playoff series deficit. The Knicks are one win away from their first Finals appearance since 1999. ECF Game 4: tomorrow, May 25, at Cleveland.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
BRUNSON: 30 POINTS.
BRIDGES: 22 POINTS.
OG: 21 POINTS.

Knicks 121, Cavaliers 108. New York leads ECF 3-0.

One more win. NBA Finals.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
KAT: 13 pts, 8 reb, 7 ast. Hart: 12 pts, 9 reb, 5 stl.

Five Knicks in double figures. Cavs had no answer.

ECF Game 4 tomorrow in Cleveland. Knicks lead 3-0.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The New York Knicks are one win away from the NBA Finals.

Jalen Brunson scored 30 points, Mikal Bridges added 22, and OG Anunoby contributed 21 as New York defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 121-108 in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference Finals on Saturday night at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse. The Knicks now lead the series 3-0 — and in the entire history of the NBA, no team has ever recovered from a 3-0 series deficit.

Karl-Anthony Towns was magnificent in a quieter way: 13 points, 8 rebounds, and 7 assists, dictating the flow of the Knicks' offense in ways that only show up in the box score if you know how to read it. Josh Hart, as always, was everywhere — 12 points, 9 rebounds, 4 assists, and 5 steals. The Cavs had Evan Mobley with 24 and Donovan Mitchell with 23, but those individual efforts were not enough against a team that played with collective purpose from start to finish.

This Knicks playoff run is extraordinary. They have won 10 straight games. Their offensive rating during the streak is 130.5 points per 100 possessions — the best among all 2026 playoff teams. They are not doing this with a superstar on par with the all-time greats. They are doing this through depth, discipline, and an uncommon willingness to share.

Game 4 is tomorrow night in Cleveland. The Knicks need one more win. ECF Game 4: Monday May 25, Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse, Cleveland, 8 PM ET, ABC. Series: Knicks lead 3-0.
facebook.image_caption:
Brunson 30. Bridges 22. OG 21. KAT 13-8-7. Hart 12-9-5 steals.

Five contributors. One result: Knicks lead the Eastern Conference Finals 3-0.

The NBA Finals drought — 27 years and counting — is almost over. One win to go.

article.headline: Brunson 30, Bridges 22, OG 21: Knicks Crush Cavaliers 121-108, Take 3-0 ECF Lead

stories.title: Thunder 123-108 Spurs — WCF Game 3, Thunder Lead 2-1
stories.angle: OKC's bench depth is the defining advantage of this series. WCF Game 4 is TONIGHT in San Antonio, 8 PM ET, NBC/Peacock.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
OKC's bench outscored San Antonio 76-23.

SGA: 26 pts, 12 ast. Perfect 12-for-12 from the line.
McCain: 24 off the bench.

Thunder lead WCF 2-1. GAME 4 TONIGHT. 8 PM ET. #Spurs

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Oklahoma City's bench is the defining story of the Western Conference Finals.

The Thunder defeated the San Antonio Spurs 123-108 in Game 3 on Friday night in San Antonio, taking a 2-1 series lead. But the final score doesn't capture the scale of OKC's bench dominance: the Thunder's reserves outscored San Antonio's bench unit 76-23. That is not a close game within the game. That is a structural mismatch.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander was excellent: 26 points, 12 assists, and a perfect 12-for-12 from the free throw line. But it was Jared McCain — the reserve guard who delivered 24 points off the bench — who may have been the actual difference-maker. OKC outscored San Antonio by 28 points with McCain on the floor. Jaylin Williams added 18 points in 22 minutes, including a key four-point play in the fourth quarter that extended a four-possession lead.

Victor Wembanyama scored 26 for the Spurs and again carried the weight of the team on his enormous frame. But the Spurs are averaging just 21.3 points per game from their bench in this series, compared to OKC's 61. That gap cannot be bridged by one player alone — not even Wembanyama.

Game 4 is tonight in San Antonio. 8 PM ET on NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption:
OKC's bench: 76 points. San Antonio's bench: 23 points.

SGA had 26 and 12 assists. McCain had 24 off the bench. Wemby scored 26 — and it still wasn't enough.

Thunder lead the WCF 2-1. Game 4 is TONIGHT.

article.headline: OKC's Bench Erupts for 76 Points as Thunder Top Spurs 123-108, Lead WCF 2-1

stories.title: WCF Game 4 TONIGHT — Thunder vs. Spurs, AT&T Center, 8 PM ET
stories.angle: After being dominated by OKC's depth in Game 3, San Antonio must find answers tonight or face elimination pressure.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
TONIGHT. San Antonio. 8 PM ET. NBC/Peacock.

WCF Game 4. OKC leads 2-1.

SGA vs. Wemby. Thunder bench vs. Spurs grit.

Can San Antonio stop the bleeding at home?

#HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
WCF Game 4 is about to tip off.

Thunder lead 2-1. Wembanyama must be special tonight.

OKC's bench has been the story of this series. Can SA fix it?

8 PM ET. NBC/Peacock.

#HoopHeroes #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Tonight's WCF Game 4 is the most critical game of the series for San Antonio.

The Oklahoma City Thunder lead 2-1 after a statement road win in Game 3. The Thunder are now looking to go up 3-1 and put themselves in position to close out the series in Oklahoma City. For the Spurs, this is effectively a must-win. Lose tonight and you are staring down the same odds Cleveland faces in the East.

The central challenge for San Antonio is clear: their bench has been outscored 76-23 in a single game by OKC's reserves. Victor Wembanyama has been outstanding — 41.7 minutes per night, 26 points in Game 3, a force on both ends — but he cannot be the only answer. Stephon Castle must be more assertive. The Spurs need bodies around Wembanyama who can score against OKC's second unit.

For Oklahoma City, the formula is working. SGA controls the game, McCain and Jalen Williams provide explosive bench minutes, and the Thunder are comfortable playing from behind. The Spurs are favored by 2.5 tonight, but the numbers say OKC is the stronger team in every category that matters.

Tip-off: 8 PM ET. AT&T Center, San Antonio. NBC/Peacock.
facebook.image_caption:
WCF Game 4 is TONIGHT.

OKC leads the series 2-1. The Thunder's bench has been dominant. Wembanyama has been brilliant — but the Spurs need more.

8 PM ET. AT&T Center, San Antonio. NBC/Peacock.

stories.title: Knicks One Win From First NBA Finals Since 1999
stories.angle: The Knicks are doing this without a single player widely considered a top-5 NBA talent. It is team basketball at its peak.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
1999. That's the last time the Knicks were in the NBA Finals.

They are ONE WIN away from ending the 27-year drought.

10 straight playoff wins. 130.5 offensive rating. No one is stopping them.

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Twenty-seven years.

That is how long New York has waited for this moment. The Knicks last appeared in the NBA Finals in 1999 — they lost to the San Antonio Spurs in five games — and since then, the franchise has cycled through stars, coaches, owners, and heartbreak. The Finals felt like a distant memory.

Tomorrow night in Cleveland, the 2026 Knicks can end that drought.

This team has won 10 straight playoff games. Their offensive rating during that run — 130.5 points per 100 possessions — is the best among all teams still competing in these playoffs. They have done it without a single player widely considered among the top five in the NBA, relying instead on depth, execution, and the kind of collective trust that takes years to build.

Jalen Brunson is the cornerstone: 30 points in Game 3, 38 in Game 1, 19 in Game 2. But what makes this team special is that Brunson doesn't have to be alone. Mikal Bridges has emerged as a star. OG Anunoby has been lockdown on defense and a genuine scoring threat. Karl-Anthony Towns orchestrates without demanding the spotlight. Josh Hart does everything the box score undervalues and then some.

One win. NBA Finals. New York, this team is for you.
facebook.image_caption:
Last NBA Finals appearance: 1999.

That drought could end tomorrow night if the Knicks beat Cleveland in Game 4. New York leads the ECF 3-0.

Ten straight playoff wins. A franchise-record postseason run. One more game to go.

article.headline: 27 Years Later: The Knicks Are One Win From the NBA Finals

stories.title: ECF Game 4 Preview — Cavaliers' Last Stand in Cleveland
stories.angle: This is Cleveland's season on the line. One more Knicks win and the series is over. Can the Cavs extend it or will New York celebrate in Ohio?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
No team has EVER come back from 3-0 in NBA playoff history.

Cleveland hosts Game 4 tomorrow. Mitchell, Mobley, and Harden must be elite.

Can the Cavs extend it or do the Knicks close out in Ohio?

#HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The odds facing Cleveland could not be steeper.

No team in NBA history has ever won a playoff series after trailing 3-0. The Cavaliers enter tomorrow's Game 4 — Monday night in Cleveland — needing not just a win, but a sequence of wins that has never been accomplished in the history of this sport.

The individual talent is there for Cleveland to compete. Donovan Mitchell scored 23 in Game 3 and has been a consistent producer throughout this series. Evan Mobley had 24 on Saturday night. These are legitimate All-Star level performances. The problem is that the supporting cast has not matched the output needed for a team to beat the 2026 Knicks.

Cleveland's home crowd will be electric tomorrow night. Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse could be the loudest it has been all season. The Cavaliers will have motivation, crowd energy, and the pride of a team that doesn't want its season to end this way.

Whether that is enough against a Knicks team that has the best offensive rating among 2026 playoff teams and has won 10 games in a row is another question entirely. Game 4: Monday May 25, Cleveland. 8 PM ET. ABC.
facebook.image_caption:
No team in NBA history has ever come back from 3-0 in the playoffs.

The Cavs host Game 4 tomorrow. Mitchell and Mobley have produced in this series — but New York is playing at another level.

Can Cleveland write history, or do the Knicks close it out in Ohio?

article.headline: ECF Game 4 Preview: Cavaliers Face Elimination, History, and an Unbeatable Knicks Streak
